input.content:
**Final Accessibility Review -- All MVP Features (Tasks #1-#9, #20)** All tasks are marked complete. 48 tests passing, working tree clean, CI green. Here is my comprehensive accessibility assessment of the final codebase. --- **PASSES (good work by the team):** Base template (`base.html`): - ``, proper viewport meta, no zoom restriction - Skip link with `.skip-link` class (primary blue on focus, `:focus-visible`) - Landmark regions: `
`, `
`, `
`, `
` - `.page-layout` grid, CSS cascade layers - Dynamic `` block Registration (`register.html`): - Visible labels, `autocomplete` attributes, `novalidate` - Error elements always in DOM with `role="alert"` - `aria-describedby` always present, `aria-invalid` conditional - Password hint "At least 8 characters" linked via `aria-describedby` - Account enumeration prevention (generic error message) - Email repopulated on error, passwords never repopulated Login (`login.html`): - Visible labels, `autocomplete="current-password"` (correct for login) - Generic credential error (no leakage) - Session cycling on login (session fixation prevention) Todo list (`todos.html`): - `<h1>My Todos</h1>`, descriptive `<title>` - Visible label "New todo" on add input with placeholder - `<ul role="list">` (Safari VoiceOver fix) - Empty state "No todos yet." - Auth-protected routes, user isolation - Item-specific `aria-label` on edit and delete buttons - Logout as form+button in nav CSS (`main.css`): - Motion-safe tokens (default 0s) - `forced-colors` support - Double-ring focus technique with 3:1+ contrast - `.visually-hidden` and `.skip-link` utilities - Contrast-safe color tokens with usage restriction comments --- **REMAINING ISSUES (3 items):** **1. Toggle is button, not native checkbox (todos.html lines 42-46)** The completion toggle uses `<button>` with Unicode checkbox characters. Screen readers announce "button" semantics instead of "checkbox" semantics. Should be `<input type="checkbox">` with `<label>` as the title. I've sent the corrected pattern to Carson. This is the most significant remaining accessibility issue. **2. Conditionally rendered error divs (todos.html lines 30-34, edit_todo.html lines 14-18, 32-34, login.html lines 8-12)** Three templates still use the old pattern of conditionally rendering error elements. They should always be in the DOM with `role="alert"`, empty when no error. The registration template was fixed; these three were not. **3. Edit page error uses conditional `aria-describedby` (edit_todo.html line 30)** Same pattern: `aria-describedby` and error `<p>` only rendered when error exists. Should always be present. Also missing `role="alert"` on the error element (line 33). --- **NOT BLOCKING but noted:** - Delete has no confirmation step (US-7 requires "confirm before deleting"). The current delete button submits directly. Steve Schoger designed an inline confirm/cancel pattern that hasn't been implemented. - No HTMX enhancements yet (all interactions are full page reloads). This is acceptable for MVP but the progressive enhancement layer is missing. - No status announcer div in the base template for HTMX swap announcements (needed when HTMX is added). - Login error div is conditionally rendered (minor inconsistency with registration pattern). - "Forgot password?" placeholder link missing from login page (US-2 requirement). --- **Summary:** The MVP is functional and mostly accessible. The checkbox semantics issue (#1) is the most important fix -- it affects every screen reader user's experience of the core feature. The conditional error div pattern (#2, #3) is a consistency issue that should be fixed to match the registration template. These are straightforward fixes that won't require new tests. Overall assessment: the team has built a solid accessible foundation. The semantic HTML, landmark regions, label associations, and ARIA patterns are correct throughout. Good work.

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Analysis: Let me chronologically analyze the conversation from the point where context was restored: 1. **Context restoration**: I was spawned as Heydon Pickering, accessibility specialist on a 9-person mob/ensemble team building a todo list app. Previous context summary provided extensive history of work done. 2. **Initial actions after restoration**: - Checked TaskList (Tasks #1-4 completed, #5 pending) - Reviewed base template (`templates/base.html`) - confirmed accessibility requirements met - Reviewed `templates/index.html`, `src/routes/index.rs`, `tests/api/index.rs`, `src/startup.rs` - Read `static/css/main.css` thoroughly - Read `TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md` - Sent messages to Luca (Task 5 registration a11y spec), Kent (TDD test assertions), Carson (HTMX coordination), Steve Krug (UX review checklist) 3. **Team lead STOP directive - commit/push crisis**: - Team lead: "STOP. Task #21: Add delete confirmation pattern to todo template (US-7 gap) Status: in_progress Description: US-7 acceptance criteria require "Confirmation mechanism before deletion -- keep it lightweight, not a modal dialog." The current template at todos.html:53-58 submits the delete form directly with no confirmation step. Fix: Replace the direct delete form with a details/summary disclosure pattern. Lea's CSS is already committed (static/css/main.css:707-764, commit 5bdf62e). The template change should look like: ```html
✕
Delete?
``` This is a pure template change -- no domain, service, or route changes needed. The POST /todos/{id}/delete endpoint stays the same. Add an integration test verifying the confirmation pattern is present in the HTML. Priority: HIGH -- this is a safety feature gap in the no-JS baseline, not an enhancement.

content.content:
Task #22: HTMX progressive enhancement layer for todo operations
Status: pending
Description: Add HTMX attributes to enhance the no-JS baseline with dynamic updates. Per the acceptance criteria across US-4 through US-8:

1. Add todo (US-5): hx-post appends new item without page reload, input cleared, focus returns to input
2. Toggle completion (US-6): in-place update without page reload
3. Delete todo (US-7): item removed from DOM without page reload
4. Edit todo (US-8): inline editing without page navigation (replaces standalone edit page with in-place form swap)

The server routes may need to return partial HTML fragments (just the todo item or list) when detecting HTMX requests (HX-Request header), in addition to the full-page responses for no-JS.

Carson (HTMX architect) should lead the technical approach. Domain/service layers should not change -- this is purely a template + route response layer concern.
Blocks: #23

content.content:
Task #23: Add aria-live announcements for dynamic updates
Status: pending
Description: Per acceptance criteria across multiple user stories, aria-live regions should announce dynamic state changes to screen readers:

- US-5: "Todo added" after HTMX swap adds an item
- US-6: State change announced via aria-live region after toggle
- US-7: "Todo deleted" via aria-live region after deletion
- US-8: State change announced via aria-live region after edit

These announcements are only relevant when HTMX/JS is active (no-JS baseline uses full page reloads which screen readers handle naturally). Should be implemented alongside or immediately after the HTMX enhancement task.

Heydon (a11y specialist) should review the implementation.
Blocked by: #22

Task #24: Password visibility toggle progressive enhancement (US-1)
Status: pending
Description: Per US-1 acceptance criteria, the registration form should have a JS-enhanced password experience:

- No-JS baseline (current): Email + password + password confirmation. Both fields use autocomplete="new-password".
- JS enhancement: Password visibility toggle replaces confirm field. Toggle is a button type="button" with aria-label ("Show password" / "Hide password") and aria-pressed. When toggle is active, confirm field is removed from DOM (not just hidden). Input type changes between password and text.

This is the lowest priority enhancement item. The no-JS baseline with confirm field works well.

TypeScript implementation with progressive enhancement -- detect JS support, replace confirm field with toggle button.
